none: Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant : environmental impact statement.

The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) proposed building a two-unit nuclear power plant in Tishomingo County, Mississippi, near the Yellow Creek embayment. The plant was intended to meet growing electricity demands in the region. The project promised economic benefits but raised concerns about environmental impacts, including potential effects on local water quality and recreation areas. The construction would convert some forest land to industrial use, and there were worries about the strain on local infrastructure and services due to an influx of workers.
Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant : environmental impact statement.

The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) proposed building a two-unit nuclear power plant in Tishomingo County, Mississippi, near the Yellow Creek embayment. The plant was intended to meet growing electricity demands in the region. The project promised economic benefits but raised concerns about environmental impacts, including potential effects on local water quality and recreation areas. The construction would convert some forest land to industrial use, and there were worries about the strain on local infrastructure and services due to an influx of workers.

The Yellow Creek Nuclear Plant was proposed by the Tennessee Valley Authority to address increasing electricity demands in the region. However, the project faced significant public pushback due to concerns about environmental impacts, particularly on water quality and local recreation areas. Ultimately, the project was canceled, reflecting the challenges of balancing energy needs with environmental protection.
"The overall appeal of the Yellow Creek area for recreational activity will be diminished by this project." — Responses to Comments from United States Department of the Interior
"We believe the NRC has failed to assess the true significance of the impacts to recreation which will result." — Responses to Comments from United States Department of the Interior
